old_claim.note: Integrated Circuits (Microchips) is modeled as a necessary component or method for this technology in the current graph.

new_claim.note: Monolithic microwave and CMOS integrated circuits enabled GPS receivers to become small, low-power, low-cost, and deployable at scale, but they are not the constitutive satellite-ranging principle.

source_shape.source_locator: Department of Defense, "Miniature Global Positioning System Receivers," printed p. 80: describes GaAs MMIC, CMOS tracker/correlator, synthesizer, microprocessor, and interface chips as the technology that miniaturized GPS receivers for lightweight, low-cost handheld use.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The federal report ties several integrated-circuit families to the transition from bulky GPS equipment to a cigarette-package-sized receiver.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Those chips materially changed receiver size, power, price, and deployability while leaving the satellite timing and radio-ranging method unchanged.

ontology_before: Integrated circuits were treated as a hard constituent of GPS itself.
ontology_after: Integrated circuits are modeled as the scaling and miniaturization path for practical GPS receivers, not as the defining ranging mechanism.

demotion_preserves.reason: The transmitting satellite constellation remains the indispensable space segment of NAVSTAR GPS.
Precise synchronized time remains the indispensable basis for GPS range measurement.
The satellite-to-receiver radio link still carries every ranging and navigation message.
Relativistic clock corrections remain necessary for the scoped accurate timing and positioning service.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-gps-navstar-logistics-boundary: integrated_circuits remains commercial_or_scaling_dependency while four constitutive GPS mechanisms remain required.
would_reject_if: Primary evidence shows that the original GPS ranging architecture could not operate at any scale without monolithic integrated circuits.
short_reason: The federal 1988 report supports chips as receiver-miniaturization and deployment technology rather than the core GPS method.
